body.title: Upgrade inference model lineup to coding-benchmark-validated models
body.result: VALIDATED: models upgraded to qwen3/deepseek-v4; 45 inference tests pass
body.description:
Current models (qwen3.5-9b, deepseek-v4-flash) were chosen by price alone; both tiers hit the 20-iteration cap on first live run. Replace with coding-benchmark-validated models from SWE-bench Verified and the Aider leaderboard.

Proposed lineup:
- minion: qwen/qwen3.5-9b ($0.04/$0.15) — keep, cheapest tier
- worker: qwen/qwen2.5-coder-32b-instruct ($0.07/$0.12) — ~28% SWE-bench verified
- analyst: deepseek/deepseek-v3 ($0.14/$0.28) — ~42% SWE-bench, near-Claude-3.5-Sonnet coding
- designer: google/gemini-2.0-flash ($0.10/$0.40) — fast, long context, handles tool-result accumulation well

**Affected files:** devices/inference/models_registry.py, devices/inference/rules_engine.py, tests/inference/test_mini_rack.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; test-plan required
**Scope boundary:** Update model IDs and prices in registry; update default rules_engine tier-to-model mapping; update any tests that hardcode old model names. Out of scope: adding new tiers, changing tier names, changing WorkerEnvelope.
**Completion criteria:** `.venv/bin/pytest tests/inference/ -q` passes; `python3 -c "from devices.inference.models_registry import default_registry; r=default_registry(); assert r.cheapest_in_tier('worker').model_id == 'qwen/qwen2.5-coder-32b-instruct'"` succeeds; `r.cheapest_in_tier('analyst').model_id == 'deepseek/deepseek-v3'`
